design_philosophy.core_loop: Select one of eight stages in any order, navigate obstacles and enemies, defeat stage boss to gain their special weapon, use weapon weaknesses to optimize boss order, collect upgrades (armor parts, chips, power-ups) to customize X's abilities
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Boss weapon weakness puzzle (determining optimal stage order) vs. execution challenge (platforming precision and enemy pattern recognition)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Tactical robot master - player as strategic commander selecting battle order, then adapting playstyle through weapon/chip customization mid-campaign
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: First in series to introduce mid-campaign character customization (armor parts, special chips) allowing players to tailor difficulty to their playstyle; introduces Zero as limited-playability alternative character, emphasizing that different tools solve problems differently rather than universal progression

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system with life energy meter; defeated stages require replay
difficulty.continues_system: Password system allowing mid-campaign saves with stage completion tracking and inventory retention
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing; mid-stage Ride Armor vehicles provide temporary respawn points within levels
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through multiple mechanics: (1) Stage selection order exploits boss weaknesses, (2) Armor part collection (head/body/legs/X-Buster) customizes offense/defense, (3) Chip selection (one active at a time) adds tactical flexibility, (4) Sub-Tanks provide healing buffers for difficult sections, (5) Zero substitution offers alternative playstyle for stuck players

progression.upgrade_system: Modular armor upgrade system (4 parts: head, body, legs, X-Buster) found in capsules throughout stages; each part provides incremental ability enhancements. Special chips provide one-active-at-a-time ability modifications. Heart Tanks extend max life. Sub-Tanks store energy. Ride Armor vehicles unlock via items for mid-stage deployment.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Defeating each of eight stage bosses grants their special weapon (weak to specific boss vulnerability). Completing four optional Doppler stages unlocks final confrontation. Zero becomes permanently unavailable if defeated, forcing commitment to resource management.
replayability_hooks: Stage order optimization based on weapon weaknesses creates multiple valid paths
Armor part collection incentivizes thorough exploration across multiple playthroughs
Chip customization creates build variety (offense/defense/utility focused builds)
Zero availability as scarce resource creates tension in using his limited capability window
Password system enables mid-campaign saves, encouraging experimentation with different stage orders

world_building_techniques: Stage theming reflects boss character identity (e.g., Toxic Seahorse stage has ocean/water hazards)
Dr. Doppler utopia framing provides context for why Mavericks reappeared despite apparent reformation
Reploid scientist authority figure (Dr. Doppler) subverts expectation that all robots are inherently dangerous
Recurring antagonist Sigma's hidden involvement (twist ending) provides narrative continuity with X1-X2

boss_design.philosophy: Each of eight stage bosses designed with singular weakness to a specific special weapon from another stage, creating interconnected dependency puzzle. Boss patterns reward both pattern memorization and weapon adaptation. Bosses are susceptible to standard X-Buster but taking specific weapon does significantly reduced damage, incentivizing optimal stage routing.
notable_bosses: Blast Hornet - insect-themed stage boss (prototype version had unused vertical boss door entry from ceiling)

faction_indicators: Maverick Hunters (X and Zero's military taskforce)
Dopple Town utopia (reformed Mavericks under Dr. Doppler)
Dr. Cain's faction (Reploid inventor and military authority)
danger_signals: Mavericks reverting to criminal behavior despite Doppler's reformation technology
Attack on Hunter headquarters by reformed Reploids signaling coordinated uprising
Sigma's hidden orchestration of Doppler crisis indicating multi-front threat
environmental_storytelling: Stage environments reflect captured Maverick boss themes (water hazard for Seahorse, electrical for Catfish)
Ride Armor vehicles represent Hunter military infrastructure for stage control
Capsule placement in dangerous zones suggests deliberate armor distribution for hunter operatives
Dopple Town iconography vs. stage decay indicates utopian promise corrupted by Maverick reversion

sources_used: tcrf - Mega Man X3 (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor [gameplay mechanics, boss doors, prototype info]
wikipedia - Mega Man X3 [gameplay systems, plot summary, character abilities, stage structure]
